title: hieracium pallescens subsp. golicae
intro: Hieracium pallescens subsp. golicae, also known as the Golica Hawkweed, is a rare plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is typically found in mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Hieracium pallescens subsp. golicae include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
